pattern: Each digit's count is fixed by its value and zero is forbidden, so the valid digit multisets form a tiny enumerable set, and for each one a digit-by-digit construction yields the smallest palindrome exceeding the target.
hints: Digit 0 can never appear (it would need to occur zero times), and a palindrome allows at most one digit with an odd count, so only a handful of digit sets are usable.
For a chosen digit set the full length is fixed; you only choose the arrangement of its first half, with the unique odd-count digit forced into the center.
For each valid set, build the smallest half-arrangement whose palindrome beats n (matching n's prefix and then going one digit higher), then take the minimum candidate across all sets.

edge_inputs.description: two-digit target needs a three-digit answer
target equals a special number, must go strictly greater
crossing from a four-of-a-kind to a longer palindrome
gap before the next six-plus-digit special

stress.description: random small targets validated against an increment-and-test oracle
